infobox.title: Insurance DApp
infobox.imageCaption: Ethereum smart contract interaction via MetaMask

lead:
The **Insurance DApp** is a decentralised insurance application I built on the **Ethereum blockchain**, using Solidity smart contracts to replace the traditional insurance company as the intermediary. Policies, premiums, claims, and payouts are all handled by on-chain logic — no human in the loop, no company deciding your claim.

I built this to explore the gap between centralised insurance (where a company decides your outcome) and decentralised insurance (where a smart contract executes pre-agreed rules automatically and transparently). It sits alongside [[foss-coin|FOSS Coin]] in my blockchain portfolio — together they cover two completely different ecosystems: Ethereum (EVM, Solidity) and Solana (SPL, Rust runtime).

The application is **live** at [insurance.lingadevaru.in](https://insurance.lingadevaru.in) — you can interact with it directly using a MetaMask wallet connected to the Sepolia testnet. No real ETH needed: grab Sepolia test ETH from any faucet, connect MetaMask, and you can purchase a policy, submit a claim, and watch the smart contract execute the payout — all on-chain, in real time.

The full source code — Solidity contracts, Truffle migration scripts, contract ABI, and the complete frontend — is open-sourced on [GitHub (github.com/lingadevaru-hp/insurance-dapp)](https://github.com/lingadevaru-hp/insurance-dapp). The repo includes a README with step-by-step setup instructions for anyone who wants to fork, deploy, or audit the contract logic.

Built with **Solidity** for the smart contracts, **Truffle** for compilation and migration, **Web3.js** for the browser-to-blockchain interaction layer, and a plain **HTML5 / CSS3 / Bootstrap** frontend — this project deliberately avoids modern JS frameworks. The goal was to understand the raw contract interaction without abstraction: every transaction is constructed manually, signed via MetaMask, and broadcast directly to the Ethereum network. No backend server. No database. No company infrastructure of any kind.

sections.title: Why Decentralised Insurance Made Sense to Build

content.content:
In traditional insurance, the insurer holds all the power: they collect premiums, evaluate claims, and decide payouts. As a claimant, you have no way to independently verify that the rules are being applied fairly — you just have to trust the company.

A smart contract flips this: the rules are written in Solidity, deployed on-chain, and execute automatically when conditions are met. Anyone can read the contract code before purchasing a policy. Payouts happen without a human decision-maker.

The trade-off I had to accept: smart contracts can't access real-world data without an oracle — and my implementation doesn't include one. I'm demonstrating the core mechanic of trustless policy execution, not shipping a production insurance product.

content.title: What this project is and isn't
content.content: This is a technical learning exercise demonstrating Solidity contract design, Truffle deployment workflow, and Web3.js/MetaMask browser integration. It is not a live insurance product and does not implement real-world oracle connections.

rows.What I used it for: Policy logic, claim rules, payout execution on Ethereum
UI for policy purchase, claim submission, payout

content.content:
The DApp follows a standard insurance lifecycle — but every step is enforced by my smart contract rather than a company employee:

1. **Policy purchase** — a user pays ETH to the contract, specifying coverage terms. The contract records the policy on-chain.
2. **Premium lock** — the ETH is held in the contract's balance, not transferred to an insurer.
3. **Claim submission** — the user submits a claim via the web interface. The contract evaluates whether it meets the pre-coded conditions.
4. **Payout** — if conditions are satisfied, the contract automatically transfers the payout from its balance to the claimant's wallet.
5. **Rejection** — if conditions aren't met, the claim is rejected on-chain. No manual override is possible — not even by me.

All state transitions are recorded as on-chain events and verifiable by any block explorer.

items.caption: Policy purchase interface — MetaMask payment flow
Claim submission and smart contract evaluation
Automatic payout transaction on the Ethereum network

content.content: The Insurance DApp is deployed and accessible online. You can interact with the live demo using a Web3-enabled browser (like Chrome with MetaMask extension). The smart contract is deployed on the Ethereum testnet (Sepolia), so no real ETH is needed — just grab some Sepolia test ETH from a faucet and try it out.

content.title: Live Demo
content.content: Visit [insurance.lingadevaru.in](https://insurance.lingadevaru.in) to see the DApp in action. You'll need MetaMask connected to the Sepolia testnet. The full source code, contract ABI, and deployment instructions are available on GitHub.

The frontend is a clean Bootstrap interface that walks through each step: connecting your wallet, viewing active policies, submitting claims, and seeing the smart contract's response in real time. Every transaction is signed through MetaMask and broadcast to the Sepolia testnet.

Everything you need to explore, verify, or fork this project is linked below. The GitHub repository contains the full source code, Truffle migration scripts, and a README with setup instructions. The live demo lets you interact with the deployed contract without cloning anything.

rows.What you get: Full source code — Solidity contracts, Truffle config, frontend HTML/CSS/JS
Interactive DApp running on Sepolia testnet — try policy purchase, claims, payouts
Verified contract code, transaction history, event logs
All my Web3 projects — FOSS Coin (Solana), this DApp (Ethereum), and more

sections.title: How This Connects to My Other Work

content.content:
This DApp and [[foss-coin|FOSS Coin]] represent the two main blockchain ecosystems I've worked with: Ethereum (EVM-based, Solidity smart contracts) and Solana (Rust-based runtime, SPL token program). Together they demonstrate breadth across different consensus mechanisms, programming models, and toolchains.

Both are listed in the [[about|GitHub repository table]] and form the blockchain/Web3 component of my technical skills. The GitHub repo is at [github.com/lingadevaru-hp/insurance-dapp](https://github.com/lingadevaru-hp/insurance-dapp).
